messages.content: When using Googlesheets and Am using the query function to only include examples where Col20 is greater than 10 however it is still returning results lower than 10, how should I fix this

To ensure that only examples where Col20 is greater than 10 are included in the query results, you need to modify the query statement. 
You can try the following query statement:

=QUERY(Sheet1!A1:T, "SELECT * WHERE T > 10")

This query will select all rows from columns A to T where the value in column T (which is equivalent to Col20) is greater than 10. 
Make sure to replace "Sheet1" with the actual name of your sheet, and adjust the column range as needed.
how do i query a sheet where I want to return all entries in Column T which are greater than 10, the entries in column T are strings and must be converted to numbers

You can use the `QUERY` function together with the `VALUE` function to convert the strings in Column T to numbers, and then filter the results by values greater than 10. Here's the formula you can use:

`=QUERY(A1:T, "SELECT * WHERE VALUE(T) > 10", 1)`

This formula selects all columns and rows in the range A1:T, and filters the results to only show rows where the value in column T (after conversion to a number using the `VALUE` function) is greater than 10. The `,1` argument at the end specifies that the formula should treat the first row as a header row, which can be useful if your data has column headers in the first row.

Note that if there are any non-numeric values in Column T that can't be converted to numbers, this formula will return an error. You can use `IFERROR` to handle such cases and replace error values with blank cells, like this:

`=IFERROR(QUERY(A1:T, "SELECT * WHERE VALUE(T) > 10", 1), "")`

This formula will return blank cells wherever an error would have occurred.
How to query a sheet full of strings of numbers (for example while a cell says 10 it is a really a string saying 10 not a number) in order to return only values greater than or equal to 10

To filter a sheet full of strings of numbers to return only values greater than or equal to 10, you can use the `QUERY` function with the `VALUE` function to convert the strings to numbers, and then apply the filter to the numbers.

Here's an example formula that demonstrates this approach:

`=QUERY(A1:T, "SELECT * WHERE VALUE(T) >= 10", 1)`

In this formula, `A1:T` is the range of cells that you want to filter. The second argument of the `QUERY` function is a query string that tells the formula what to do. In this case, `"SELECT * WHERE VALUE(T) >= 10"` means "select all rows and columns where the value in column T is greater than or equal to 10". The `VALUE` function converts the string values to numbers.

The `,1` at the end of the formula tells `QUERY` to treat the first row as a header row. You can use this if your sheet has headers in the first row.

Make sure that all cells in column T contain string values that can be converted to numbers. If there are any cells that contain non-numeric strings, you may encounter errors when using the `VALUE` function.

If you want to return only the values in column T that are greater than or equal to 10, you can modify the query to select only that column:

`=QUERY(A1:T, "SELECT T WHERE VALUE(T) >= 10", 1)` 

In this case, the query selects only column T and returns only the rows where the value in T is greater than or equal to 10.

How to query a sheet full of strings of numbers (for example while a cell says 10 it is a really a string saying 10 not a number) in order to return only values greater than or equal to 10 in Googlesheets

To query a sheet full of strings of numbers where the cells say 10 (as a string) and not 10 (as a number), and return only values greater than or equal to 10, you need to use the `VALUE` function to convert the strings to numbers.

Here's an example formula that demonstrates this approach:

`=QUERY(A1:T, "SELECT * WHERE VALUE(T) >= 10", 1)`

In this formula, `A1:T` is the range of cells that you want to filter. The second argument of the `QUERY` function is a query string that tells the formula what to do. In this case, `"SELECT * WHERE VALUE(T) >= 10"` means "select all rows and columns where the value in column T is greater than or equal to 10". The `VALUE` function converts the string values to numbers.

The `,1` at the end of the formula tells `QUERY` to treat the first row as a header row. You can use this if your sheet has headers in the first row.

If you want to return only the values in column T that are greater than or equal to 10, you can modify the query to select only that column:

`=QUERY(A1:T, "SELECT T WHERE VALUE(T) >= 10", 1)`

In this case, the query selects only column T and returns only the rows where the value in T is greater than or equal to 10.

Note that you may encounter errors if there are any cells in column T that cannot be converted to numbers using the `VALUE` function. If you encounter such errors, you can wrap the `VALUE` function in an `IFERROR` statement to handle them. For example:

`=QUERY(A1:T, "SELECT * WHERE IFERROR(VALUE(T), 0) >= 10", 1)`

This formula will treat any non-numeric strings in column T as 0, so you won't get an error when using the `VALUE` function.
That doesn't work, it returns an error still

I'm sorry to hear that. If you're still experiencing issues, try this formula to see if it works to filter values greater than or equal to 10 that are stored as strings in a given range:

```
=QUERY(A1:T, "SELECT * WHERE T >= '10'")
```

In this formula, `A1:T` is the range of cells you want to filter.

The query string `"SELECT * WHERE T >= '10'"` means select all rows and columns where the value in column T is greater than or equal to 10. Since the value you're comparing against is itself a string, you need to use single quotes to enclose it.

This formula should work as long as there are no non-numeric characters in Column T. However, it is important to note that this formula will treat values as text, so if you have a mix of numbers and text in Column T, you may get unexpected results. If you need to treat the values in Column T as numbers, try formatting the column as a number first, then apply the `QUERY` formula with `>=10`.

How to query to return all the data from Column A,B, and F where the values in Column G are greater than 5 and Column A does not contain the word 'Phantasmal', The data is in a different sheet titled 'Bulk Data' in the same workbook. In googlesheets

You can use the `QUERY` function along with the `AND` and `NOT` functions to filter the data in the 'Bulk Data' sheet, and return the values from columns A, B, and F where Column G has a value greater than 5.

Here's the formula that should work for you:

```
=QUERY('Bulk Data'!A:F, "SELECT A, B, F WHERE NOT A contains 'Phantasmal' AND G > 5",1)
```
In this formula `'Bulk Data'!A:F` refers to a range that includes columns A to F for sheet titled 'Bulk Data'

The `SELECT A, B, F` statement will return only the values in columns A, B, and F.

The `WHERE NOT A contains 'Phantasmal' AND G > 5` statement will filter the rows based on the two conditions. The `NOT A contains 'Phantasmal'` condition filters out rows where the value in column A contains the word 'Phantasmal'. The `G > 5` condition filters out rows where the value in column G is less than or equal to 5.

The `,1` at the end of the formula tells the `QUERY` function to treat the first row as a header row. You can use this if your sheet has headers in the first row.

Make sure to adjust the sheet name and column ranges as needed, based on the location of the data in your workbook.
Query hide results lower than 10

To filter a range of cells to hide results lower than 10 using `QUERY` function in Google Sheets, you can add a `WHERE` clause to the query that checks for values greater than or equal to 10, and then use the `SELECT` clause to return only the columns you want to display.

Here's an example formula:

```
=QUERY(A1:D, "SELECT A, B, C, D WHERE D>=10", 1)
```

In this formula, `A1:D` is the range of cells that you want to filter. The second argument of the `QUERY` function is the query string that tells the formula what to do.

The `"SELECT A, B, C, D"` clause specifies which columns to include in the resulting table. You can change the column letters to match the columns you want to display.

The `"WHERE D>=10"` clause filters the results to show only rows where the value in column D is greater than or equal to 10.

The `,1` at the end of the formula tells the `QUERY` function to treat the first row as a header row. You can use this if your sheet has headers in the first row.

Note that if there are non-numeric values in column D that cannot be compared to 10, you may encounter errors or unexpected results. In this case, you may need to remove those values or convert them to numbers before using the `QUERY` function.

the function is have is =QUERY(ImportJSON("https://poe.ninja/api/data/itemoverview?league=Crucible&type=SkillGem"),"Select Col2, Col17, Col19, Col20 where Col5='20/20' AND not Col2 contains 'Phantasmal' Limit 15",1) this imports the json data from the url https://poe.ninja/api/data/itemoverview?league=Crucible&type=SkillGem and returns Columns 2,17,19, and 20 stylized as Col2, Col17, Col19, and Col20, It also only returns cells where the value in Col5 equals the string '20/20' and where Col2 does not contain the word Phantasmal, How can I change the function to also only return where the value of Col20 is greater than 10 in googlesheets

You can add an additional clause to the `WHERE` statement to filter the results based on the value of Col20. Here's the modified formula:

```
=QUERY(ImportJSON("https://poe.ninja/api/data/itemoverview?league=Crucible&type=SkillGem"),"SELECT Col2, Col17, Col19, Col20 WHERE Col5='20/20' AND NOT Col2 CONTAINS 'Phantasmal' AND Col20 > 10 LIMIT 15", 1)
```

In this modified formula, the additional clause `AND Col20 > 10` has been added to the `WHERE` statement. This will filter the results to include only rows where the value in Col20 is greater than 10.

Note that you can adjust the number of rows returned using the `LIMIT` clause. In this case, the `LIMIT 15` statement limits the results to the first 15 rows meeting the specified criteria. You can adjust this number or remove the `LIMIT` statement altogether to show all matching rows.
When using that command the query is still returning values smaller than 10 (for example 2, 4, 8, etc)

If your query is still returning values smaller than 10 even after adding the condition to filter based on Col20 being greater than 10, then it's possible that the values in Col20 are being treated as strings and not numbers. To fix this, you can convert the values in Col20 to numbers using the `VALUE` function.

Here's the revised formula:

```
=QUERY(ImportJSON("https://poe.ninja/api/data/itemoverview?league=Crucible&type=SkillGem"),"Select Col2, Col17, Col19, Col20 where Col5='20/20' AND not Col2 contains 'Phantasmal' AND VALUE(Col20) > 10 Limit 15",1)
```

In this modified formula, I added `VALUE(Col20)` in the clause that filters based on Col20 being greater than 10. This will convert the values in Col20 from strings to numbers before doing the comparison.

Note that if there are any non-numeric values in Col20, this will result in an error. If you encounter that error, you can use the `IFERROR` function to handle it, like this:

```
=QUERY(ImportJSON("https://poe.ninja/api/data/itemoverview?league=Crucible&type=SkillGem"),"Select Col2, Col17, Col19, Col20 where Col5='20/20' AND not Col2 contains 'Phantasmal' AND IFERROR(VALUE(Col20),0) > 10 Limit 15",1)
```

This will treat any non-numeric values in Col20 as 0, which should eliminate the error.
that formula returns at error "Unable to parse query string for Function QUERY parameter 2: PARSE_ERROR: Encountered " <ID> "IFERROR "" at line 1, column 92. Was expecting one of: "(" ... "(" ..."

I apologize for the confusion. I made a mistake when writing the previous answer. In Google Sheets, you cannot use `IFERROR` function inside a `QUERY` function. Instead, you can use the `VALUE` function with an `IF` function to handle the possible error.

Here's an example:

```
=QUERY(ImportJSON("https://poe.ninja/api/data/itemoverview?league=Crucible&type=SkillGem"),"Select Col2, Col17, Col19, Col20 where Col5='20/20' AND not Col2 contains 'Phantasmal' AND IFERROR(VALUE(Col20), 0) > 10 LIMIT 15",1)
```

In this formula, the `IF` function handles any non-numeric values in Col20, by replacing them with 0. That way, the query can handle the filter condition `AND VALUE(Col20) > 10` without throwing an error.

Note that if there are still any non-numeric values in Col20, this will result in the expected value of zero, so you may need to adjust the filter condition to exclude those cases.

I hope this helps!

I removed the iferror and i still get an error "Unable to parse query string for Function QUERY parameter 2: PARSE_ERROR: Encountered " <ID> "VALUE "" at line 1, column 92. Was expecting one of: "(" ... "(" ..."

I apologize for the confusion. The issue is that when you apply the `VALUE` function inside a `QUERY` statement that uses `ImportJSON`, the string values in Col20 may not all be in the expected format to be converted to numbers.

You can try using a combination of `REGEXMATCH` and `REGEXEXTRACT` functions to extract the numeric values in Col20 and convert them to numbers. The regex pattern `(\d+(\.\d+)?)` matches any decimal number with optional decimal places in a given string.

Here's the updated formula with this approach:

```
=QUERY(ImportJSON("https://poe.ninja/api/data/itemoverview?league=Crucible&type=SkillGem"), "SELECT Col2, Col17, Col19, Col20 WHERE Col5='20/20' AND NOT Col2 CONTAINS 'Phantasmal' AND VALUE(REGEXEXTRACT(Col20, '(\d+(\.\d+)?)')) > 10 LIMIT 15",1)
```

In this formula, the `REGEXEXTRACT` function extracts the numeric values from Col20 and applies the `VALUE` function to convert them to numbers. This will filter the results based on the numeric values in Col20 being greater than 10.

Please note that this formula does not handle the case where Col20 contains non-numeric values. If there are any non-numeric values in Col20, this formula may return an error.
I want to query a sheet to pull the 15 highest entries from column B but I only want to return entires where Column C is greater than 10 however all the entries in column C are strings or numbers

You can use the `QUERY` function to filter a range based on values in different columns. In this case, to filter based on values in Column C being greater than 10, you need to use the `VALUE` function to convert the string values to numbers.

Here's an example formula that filters data in Column B and Column C and returns the top 15 results:

```
=QUERY(A1:C, "SELECT B, C WHERE C > 10 ORDER BY C DESC LIMIT 15", 1)
```

In this formula, `A1:C` is the range of cells that you want to filter.

The `"SELECT B, C WHERE C > 10"` statement will return the values in Column B and Column C where the value in Column C is greater than 10.

The `"ORDER BY C DESC"` statement sorts the rows based on the values in Column C in descending order. 

The `"LIMIT 15"` statement limits the results to the top 15 rows.

The `,1` at the end of the formula tells the `QUERY` function to treat the first row as a header row. You can use this if your sheet has headers in the first row.

Note that if there are any non-numeric values in Column C that cannot be converted to numbers using the `VALUE` function, you may encounter an error in the formula. In this case, you may need to convert those values to numbers first, or remove those rows from the data range.
